text: The surgeon begins step one by dissecting in an anticlockwise direction from the pulmonary ligament zone, maintaining exposure for the pulmonary ligament section and lymphadenectomy of lymph nodes at station nine. In step two, the posterior hilum is exposed, providing access for dissection of the posterior side of the left vein and mediastinal artery, while performing lymph node dissection at stations VII and X.

The surgeon accesses the upper hilum side, exposing the superior side of the mediastinal artery and performing lymphadenectomy at stations ten, five, and six. The lung is then retracted towards the posterior pleural side to access the anterior hilum, allowing dissection of the mediastinal artery and lymph nodes at stations ten and eleven.

The surgeon performs a complete lymph node dissection at stations eleven and twelve, controlling the artery, vein, and bronchus using robotic instruments before proceeding with the sectioning of these structures.

The surgeon performs a lymphadenectomy after cutting the bronchus to access the artery. Aerostasis is achieved by applying a nail veil sheet on the sutured lung zone. The resected specimen is placed into an angiobag for extraction. A pleural chest tube is inserted as the final step of the procedure.
